news.headline: US strikes Iran for 2nd day; technical talks resume, crude holds flat at $72
news.what_happened: US military conducted airstrikes on Iran for a second consecutive day. However, US-Iran technical negotiations were confirmed as resuming later, reducing near-term escalation fears. WTI crude held at ~$72.26/bbl (+0.25%) despite Middle East tensions.
news.why_it_matters: Crude staying flat despite geopolitical flare-up signals market views supply disruption risk as contained. Resumption of talks could push crude lower — a tailwind for India's CAD and import-heavy sectors. Tail risk remains: any strike on Iran's Kharg Island export terminal could spike crude sharply.

news.headline: TCS Q1 FY27: Revenue +14% YoY in INR, but constant currency growth only +0.4%
news.what_happened: TCS reported Q1 FY27 results on July 9: Revenue ₹72,275 Cr (+14% YoY), Net Profit ₹13,349 Cr (+5% YoY). Constant currency growth was only 0.4%. Deal wins: $9.5 billion. AI revenue at $2.6B annualized run rate. Interim dividend ₹12/share declared.
news.why_it_matters: The 14% INR growth is currency-inflated (USD/INR at 95.37 vs ~84 a year ago). CC growth of 0.4% reveals soft underlying demand. Strong deal wins and AI revenue signal future visibility. IT sector to trade mixed — HCLTECH may attract rotation as it lacks TCS's CC narrative overhang.

news.headline: Nasdaq surges 1.30%, S&P 500 +0.81% on semiconductor rebound; Micron +4.5%
news.what_happened: US markets rallied on July 9 led by semiconductors. Nasdaq gained 1.30% to 26,206.89. S&P 500 gained 0.81% to 7,543.64. Micron Technology surged 4.5%, SMH (semiconductor ETF) +2.5%, Sandisk +7.6%. Dow added 0.27% to 52,487.
news.why_it_matters: Strong Nasdaq directly re-rates Indian IT sector via valuation multiple expansion. FII sentiment for Indian IT improves. HCLTECH, WIPRO, TECHM likely to attract buying. Banking stocks also benefit from S&P strength and global risk-on mood.

news.headline: Asia rallies strongly: Nikkei +1.72%, Hang Seng +1.43% on tech optimism and US-Iran de-escalation
news.what_happened: Asian markets rose sharply on July 10. Nikkei 225 gained 1.72% to 68,912 (SoftBank +7%, chip stocks +4%+). Hang Seng surged 1.43% to 24,373. South Korea Kospi climbed 2.6% (SK Hynix, Samsung strong). MSCI Asia Pacific +0.5%.
news.why_it_matters: Broad Asia risk-on is a positive macro backdrop for Indian equities. Hang Seng strength signals improving China sentiment, supporting metals. Strong Asia tape could offset GIFT Nifty's muted -0.10% signal once Indian markets open.

news.headline: IMF cuts India FY27 GDP forecast to 6.4% citing energy prices; raises FY28 to 6.7%
news.what_happened: The IMF lowered India's FY27 growth forecast from 6.5% to 6.4%, citing higher energy prices from West Asia tensions as a headwind, while raising FY28 projection to 6.7%. ADB also cut India's FY27 forecast to 6.6%. India remains the world's fastest-growing major economy.
news.why_it_matters: Mild negative for macro sentiment. Elevated energy costs from US-Iran conflict are the key risk to India's growth. However, the FY28 upgrade and India's structural growth story remain intact. Markets likely to shrug off this marginally.

sector_outlook.reason: Nasdaq +1.30%, semiconductor rally; HCLTECH pre-market +0.42% showing strength vs TCS/INFY weakness on CC concerns
S&P 500 +0.81%, US 10Y yields eased to 4.539%, strong DII buying ₹2,058 Cr; FII selling ₹533 Cr is headwind
Hang Seng +1.43%, Nikkei +1.72%; Asia risk-on and China export resilience support demand narrative
Crude only +0.25%, below 0.5% trigger; no directional catalyst today
Crude flat at $72.26; geopolitical uncertainty but no margin impact crystallised
USD/INR essentially flat (-0.01%); no FDA events or specific catalysts identified
US 10Y yields eased to 4.539% from 4.569%; domestic DII support; RBI rate unchanged at 5.25%
No budget/capex announcement today; wait for fresh triggers

picks.catalyst: Nasdaq +1.30% overnight semiconductor and tech rally; HCLTECH pre-market showing resilience (+0.42%) while TCS/INFY are under pressure due to TCS's weak 0.4% constant currency growth narrative
picks.logic: HCL Tech earns ~55% of revenues from North America in USD. Nasdaq's 1.30% gain driven by semiconductor rebound signals valuation re-rating for Indian IT. Unlike TCS (CC headwind) and INFY (sympathy selling, own results pending), HCLTECH lacks that specific overhang today — market already rotating within IT to HCLTECH. With USD/INR at 95.37 (a historically weak rupee), any USD revenue growth translates to outsized INR gains. HCL's own Q1 results pending and expected to be solid given strong deal environment. R:R = 3.5% target / 1.5% stop = 2.3:1.

risks: Thursday = weekly F&O expiry: elevated intraday volatility — avoid chasing gap-ups at open; wait for 15-min price discovery
US-Iran conflict tail risk: any strike on Kharg Island export terminal could spike crude >5%, hurting India macro and triggering broad sell-off
TCS Q1 CC growth of only 0.4% may trigger broad IT sector selling (including HCLTECH) — watch first 15 min carefully before entering
FII continued selling: if FII selling accelerates >₹1,000 Cr, large-cap support erodes despite DII buying
IMF FY27 downgrade and elevated energy prices are macro overhangs — any fresh escalation in West Asia amplifies these risks
